summary: Joe Reis argues that the tech and data industry has a troubling tendency to forget its pioneers and foundational history, unlike other professions that revere their contributors. Understanding the historical evolution of technologies—from perceptrons to transformers—provides crucial context for comprehending current innovations like generative AI.
key_points: Tech and data industries discard their legends too quickly compared to other professions
Understanding history reveals the 'why' behind current technologies and practices
Modern innovations like LLMs are built on decades of prior work (perceptrons → neural networks → deep learning → transformers)
Knowing the lineage of ideas helps practitioners better understand and work with current tools
money_quotes: Unlike other professions that revere their contributors (think medicine - Hippocrates, Pasteur, etc.), tech and data throw out the legends as soon as it's convenient.
History is important. It helps set the context of the why and what of your profession.
Every innovation you're currently working with happened because people wanted to improve the status quo.
If you understand the building blocks of machine learning, you'll have a much easier time understanding generative AI. It didn't spring out of nowhere.
key_insight: Understanding the historical lineage of technologies—from foundational concepts to modern innovations—gives practitioners deeper insight into their field and prevents the industry from repeating past mistakes.
